summary: Soundboard source with minor pitch quirks captures a dazzling first set (Dancin', He Was A Friend of Mine, China Cat Sunflower, New Potato Caboose) while the second‑set Lovelight drags.
review: The upgraded soundboard transfer is generally clear and well balanced, though listeners note a slight speed/pitch error on the opening Dancing in the Street that needs correction. Musically the show shines in its first half – Jerry’s vocal work, Phil’s wild bass chords and the seamless China Cat → New Potato Caboose flow are repeatedly praised – but the guest‑led Lovelight portion of the second set is widely regarded as a low point.

ai_rating.rationale: Average star rating across nine reviews is 4.0; most reviewers are enthusiastic about the performance and sound quality, with only a few strong negative comments.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Clear, well‑balanced SBD transfer but suffers from a slight pitch/speed error on Dancing (requires +1–2% correction) and occasional low guest vocal levels.

standout_songs: He Was A Friend of Mine
show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is tight, psychedelic and full of memorable transitions; second set is uneven due to guest‑driven Lovelight but recovers with the Cryptical Envelopment → The Other One → Cosmic Charlie sequence.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for expressive vocals (especially on He Was A Friend of Mine) and dynamic guitar tone, though a few note his voice creaking late in Dancin' and occasional out‑of‑tune moments.
band_member_comments.Phil: Highlighted for wild bass chords, blazing tone, and unique “Patricia” jam during New Potato Caboose; several reviewers call his playing a peak of the night.
band_member_comments.Bob: Noted for sweet three‑part harmonies on He Was A Friend of Mine and overall vocal support.
